subtopics.title: Characteristics of Living Things
common_misconceptions: Plants don't move (they move towards light, water)
Growth only means getting bigger (also includes development)
All living things reproduce the same way
subtopics.teaching_note: MRS GREN: Movement, Respiration, Sensitivity, Growth, Reproduction, Excretion, Nutrition. Living things are organized: cells → tissues → organs → systems → organism. Examples: animals, plants, fungi, bacteria. Non-living: rocks, water, air, plastic. Differences: living things need food/water/air, can reproduce, respond to stimuli, have lifespan. Nigerian context: diverse flora and fauna - tropical forests, savanna animals (lions, elephants in Yankari), domestic animals.

subtopics.title: States of Matter
common_misconceptions: Air is not matter (it has mass and occupies space)
Solids cannot change shape at all (they can be broken)
Boiling and evaporation are the same
subtopics.teaching_note: Particle theory: All matter is made of tiny particles. Solids: fixed shape, fixed volume, particles vibrate in fixed positions. Liquids: fixed volume, takes shape of container, particles move past each other. Gases: no fixed shape or volume, particles move freely, spread out. Changes of state: Melting (solid → liquid), Freezing (liquid → solid), Evaporation (liquid → gas at surface), Boiling (liquid → gas throughout), Condensation (gas → liquid), Sublimation (solid → gas, e.g., naphthalene, dry ice).
